steps.name: api_keys table with Fernet encryption at rest
Security regression test — plaintext never surfaces (BR-1, permanent)
Wire up remaining tests + verify all Acceptance Criteria

decisions: 2026-07-17 reconciliation: main has no api_keys model/service/routes. Real implementation (models/api_key.py, services/api_key_service.py, core/crypto.py Fernet, api/admin/api_keys.py, security regression tests) exists only in worker-B's unpushed worktree. origin/feat/3-4-api-key-management exists but its diff vs. current main looks like a stale/pre-merge snapshot, not the real work. Blocked pending worker-B splitting/pushing.

2026-07-17 (later): by session start the real backend implementation (steps 1-6, 8-9) was already merged to main by an earlier reconciliation pass -- this state file was stale. Fixed (branch feat/3-4-fernet-key-mask-fix, commit 901e4ab, merged to main at 394bd38): (1) FERNET_MASTER_KEY test fixture across 5 test files was NOT a valid 32-byte urlsafe-base64 key (was 36 decoded bytes) -- replaced with a real Fernet.generate_key() output everywhere. (2) mask() in app/core/crypto.py didn't match the AC's tested boundary behavior for very short/degenerate inputs (len==4 vs len<4) -- fixed with documented edge-case rationale. (3) get_plaintext() was needlessly `async def` (decrypt() does no I/O) while its only caller (api_keys.py router) already called it without awaiting -- test bug (missing await) vs. real bug (wrongly-async signature); fixed by making it sync to match actual usage. (4) track_usage() had a real bug: `row.usage_count = ApiKey.usage_count + 1` used the SQLAlchemy class-level Column expression instead of the instance value, producing a BinaryExpression instead of an int -- fixed to `row.usage_count + 1`. (5) app/api/admin/api_keys.py imported KeyService/get_settings/get_adapter_class *inside* each route handler 'to avoid a circular import' that doesn't actually exist (api_key_service.py has no import back to the router) -- this silently broke every @patch("app.api.admin.api_keys.X") in the test suite; moved all three to module-level imports. (6) `_get_session(request)` returns an already-invoked session CM, but was being passed directly as `session_factory` to KeyService, which then calls it AGAIN internally (`await self._session_factory()`) expecting a callable -- real bug (`TypeError: '_CM' object is not callable" wherever it fired); fixed every `KeyService(_get_session(request))` call site to `KeyService(lambda: _get_session(request))`. Also fixed several test-file-only bugs: session.get/refresh/delete mocked as MagicMock instead of AsyncMock (AsyncSession's methods are actually async); session.scalars mocked via session.execute (service calls session.scalars() directly per SQLAlchemy 2.0 API); a provider-filter test compared literal 'gemini' against str(stmt) which renders bound params as placeholders (needed compile(literal_binds=True)); a private test helper (_make_svc_with_last_key) was both @patch-decorated AND called with an explicit mock kwarg by its callers, causing a duplicate-argument TypeError.

NOT verified this session (out of scope for this fix pass, flagged for follow-up): Step 7 (Admin > API Keys tab UI) -- no frontend work was touched or exercised in a real browser per rules/testing.md; tests/integration/test_api_keys_api.py::TestAPIKeysRBAC::test_creator_cannot_access_api_keys still fails -- root cause traced to tests/conftest.py's _build_app() always resolving a hard-coded ADMIN_USER via FakeDatabase regardless of the JWT subject/role actually presented, so the RBAC gate is never exercised with a non-admin identity in this specific integration test (a shared test-fixture gap, not a production security bug -- get_current_user's real DB-backed lookup path is unaffected). Fixing this needs a shared conftest.py change with a wider blast radius than this task's scope.
